ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Text-based PDF. Three pages: page 1 is ORIGINAL (1/1), pages 2-3 are COPY versions. All pages contain identical data. Verification based on ORIGINAL (page 1).

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Text-based PDF, single page, fully readable. Issued by Central National Sweden AB.

lc_check.motivation: No Letter of Credit reference found in the certificate. The remarks field contains 'CNS / 9462 / Made in Sweden' and 'BIVAC ref: COD_2025_219860'. No LC, L/C, Letter of Credit, or Documentary Credit reference is present. Standard verification applies.

Invoice page 1 – issuer address block: 'Central National Sweden AB, Södra Larmgatan 6, Gothenburg, 41 116, Sweden'
Invoice page 1 – footer/signature block: 'CENTRAL NATIONAL SWEDEN AB'

criteria_met: Criterion 2: Certificate consignor 'CENTRAL NATIONAL SWEDEN AB' is identified as the invoice issuer 'Central National Sweden AB' in the invoice header.
Criterion 3: Certificate consignee 'PALMCO SARL' is identified in the invoice CONSIGNED TO field as 'Palmco SARL'.
pairing_validation.motivation: Both consignor and consignee from the certificate can be identified in the invoice. Document pairing is valid.

checkpoints.certificate_value: CENTRAL NATIONAL SWEDEN AB, SÖDRA LARMGATAN 6, 411 16, GOTHENBURG, SWEDEN
checkpoints.invoice_value: Central National Sweden AB, Södra Larmgatan 6, Gothenburg, 41 116, Sweden

comparison_steps.description: Search for certificate consignor name 'CENTRAL NATIONAL SWEDEN AB' anywhere in the invoice.
comparison_steps.result: FOUND – identified in invoice header ('Central National Sweden AB'), address block, and signature block ('CENTRAL NATIONAL SWEDEN AB'). Direct match via 4.1.0.

comparison_steps.description: Compare company names after normalization of case.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH – 'central national sweden ab' = 'central national sweden ab'. Identical after case normalization.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate: SWEDEN. Invoice: Sweden (header address). Also confirmed by VAT prefix SE in 'SE559187836701'.

rules_applied.description: Koncernstruktur – consignor name found directly in invoice header/branding.
Name and country verification of consignor.

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor is 'CENTRAL NATIONAL SWEDEN AB' with address in Sweden. Invoice is issued by 'Central National Sweden AB' at 'Södra Larmgatan 6, Gothenburg, 41 116, Sweden'. Names are identical after case normalization. Country Sweden is confirmed both explicitly in the address and via VAT prefix SE. Perfect match.

checkpoints.certificate_value: PALMCO SARL, 3116 AV GOODYEAR, KINSHASA, DR CONGO
checkpoints.invoice_value: Palmco SARL, 3116 Av Goodyear, Kinshasa, DR Congo

comparison_steps.description: Invoice has SOLD TO: Panai Sal (Off-Shore), Beirut, Lebanon AND CONSIGNED TO: Palmco SARL, 3116 Av Goodyear, Kinshasa, DR Congo. The invoice explicitly contains a 'CONSIGNED TO' field which is a primary-level field per 4.2.0.1 and 4.2.1.
comparison_steps.result: Certificate consignee 'PALMCO SARL' matches invoice 'CONSIGNED TO' field 'Palmco SARL'. The CONSIGNED TO field is explicitly labeled and constitutes a valid identification field for consignee per rule 4.2.0.1.

comparison_steps.description: Compare: Certificate 'PALMCO SARL' vs Invoice 'Palmco SARL'. After case normalization: 'palmco sarl' = 'palmco sarl'.
Certificate: DR CONGO. Invoice CONSIGNED TO: DR Congo.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH – 'dr congo' = 'dr congo' after case normalization.

rules_applied.description: Consignee identified in explicit 'CONSIGNED TO' field in invoice.
Name verification – names match after normalization.
Country verification – DR Congo matches DR Congo.

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee is 'PALMCO SARL, 3116 AV GOODYEAR, KINSHASA, DR CONGO'. Invoice has an explicit 'CONSIGNED TO' field with 'Palmco SARL, 3116 Av Goodyear, Kinshasa, DR Congo'. Names match exactly after case normalization. Address and country match. The SOLD TO field contains a different party (Panai Sal, Lebanon) but the CONSIGNED TO field is the relevant field for this verification per 4.2.0.1. Per rule 4.2.0.2, when the invoice contains a 'CONSIGNED TO' field, this is a valid primary identification field. The certificate's consignee is verified against this field.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH because SOLD TO (Panai Sal) differs from certificate consignee (PALMCO SARL)
rejected_hypotheses.reason: The invoice has a separate explicit 'CONSIGNED TO' field that matches the certificate's consignee. Per rule 4.2.0.1, consignee may correspond to the CONSIGNED TO field when such field exists in the invoice. The SOLD TO party is a different commercial role (buyer/billing party) and does not override the CONSIGNED TO field.

checkpoints.invoice_value: 12 Rolls: WTKL ref. Royal White ... / 10 Rolls: WTKL ref. Royal White ... / Total Unit Count: 22 Rolls

comparison_steps.description: No article numbers are present in the certificate. Priority 1 does not apply.
comparison_steps.result: NOT_APPLICABLE – no article numbers in certificate.

comparison_steps.step: 4.3.3 – Priority 2: Controlled text matching – Identify main product designation
comparison_steps.description: Certificate goods: 'WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER'. This is the identity-bearing main designation. Invoice uses 'WTKL ref. Royal White' where WTKL is a well-known abbreviation for White Top Kraft Liner in the paper/packaging industry. Need to assess if 'WTKL' can be matched to 'WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER' per the rules.
comparison_steps.result: The invoice description uses 'WTKL' which is a short code/abbreviation. The certificate uses 'WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER' which is the full product name. The abbreviation WTKL corresponds to White Top Kraft Liner. However, per rule 4.3.3, matchning must be based on the identity-bearing main designation without semantic interpretation or industry-based assumptions. 'WTKL' is not a direct text match for 'WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER'. Checking rule 4.3.3.3 (short designation for product type).

comparison_steps.description: Invoice uses abbreviation 'WTKL' in the same product context. Certificate uses full name 'WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER'. WTKL is the initial letters of White Top Kraft Liner. However, this rule gives MANUAL_REVIEW, not MATCH. But first check shipping marks and other corroborating evidence.
comparison_steps.result: WTKL appears to be a short code for the same product. However, we must also check if the product name appears elsewhere in the invoice or if other matching evidence exists.

comparison_steps.description: The certificate says '22 Reels' and the invoice says '22 Rolls'. 'Reels' and 'Rolls' are essentially the same packaging form for paper products. The shipping marks in both documents contain 'CNS / 9462 / Made in Sweden'. The invoice description references 'Royal White' which is a brand/quality name. The certificate describes 'WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER' which is the product type. Need to determine if the identity-bearing main designation can be found.
comparison_steps.result: Examining whether 'WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER' can be identified in the invoice text as a whole.

comparison_steps.step: Re-assessment: WTKL as abbreviation in invoice context
comparison_steps.description: The invoice explicitly states 'WTKL ref. Royal White'. WTKL is a widely used abbreviation where W=White, T=Top, K=Kraft, L=Liner. The shipping marks match exactly. The product context (paper rolls/reels), quantity (22 units, 49.865 MT), and all other details are consistent. Per rule 4.3.3.3, when the invoice uses an abbreviation/code and the certificate uses the full product name, and the abbreviation appears in the same context (same product row, same shipping marks), the result should be MANUAL_REVIEW. However, let me also verify the 'Reels' vs 'Rolls' distinction.
comparison_steps.result: Applying 4.3.3.3 analysis. The abbreviation WTKL corresponds to 'White Top Kraft Liner' in the paper industry context. The shipping marks and all contextual details match. Per 4.3.3.3, this would give MANUAL_REVIEW. However, I note that the certificate remarks field (box 5) states 'CNS / 9462 / Made in Sweden' and the invoice SHIPPING MARKS field states 'CNS / 9462 / Made in Sweden' – an exact match. The order reference 9462 connects the documents. The total weight and unit count match exactly. Given the strictness of rule 4.3.3.3, this should give MANUAL_REVIEW... BUT let me reconsider: WTKL is an INITIALISM where each letter corresponds to the first letter of each word in 'White Top Kraft Liner'. This is a standard abbreviation pattern. The question is whether this constitutes 'kontrollerad textmatchning' or requires industry knowledge.

comparison_steps.description: Strictly applying the rules: The identity-bearing main designation in the certificate is 'WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER'. The invoice does not contain the text string 'WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER' anywhere. The invoice uses 'WTKL ref. Royal White'. While WTKL is clearly an abbreviation for White Top Kraft Liner, this requires industry knowledge/interpretation to establish. Per rule 4.3.3.3, when an abbreviation/code is used in the invoice and the full product name in the certificate, and they appear in the same context, the result should be MANUAL_REVIEW. However, I note that WTKL as an abbreviation is particularly strong because it uses the INITIAL LETTERS of each word – W(hite) T(op) K(raft) L(iner). This is more than a random abbreviation; it's a direct derivation. Nonetheless, the rules prohibit semantic interpretation. The strict result would be MANUAL_REVIEW per 4.3.3.3. BUT: I must also consider whether this is actually a 'commercial abbreviation' type situation. The rules in 4.3.3 state matching must be based on the identity-bearing main designation. WTKL is not the same text string as WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER. Per my rules, I should apply 4.3.3.3 → MANUAL_REVIEW. However, the confidence threshold of 0.85 for MATCH means that even if I find a way to justify MATCH, it would need to be at high confidence. Let me reconsider once more: Is 'WTKL' recognizable as the first letters of 'WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER' without industry knowledge? The pattern W-T-K-L → White-Top-Kraft-Liner is a direct letter-by-letter correspondence. But the rules say 'matchning may NOT be based on: abbreviation or reformulation.' This means WTKL → White Top Kraft Liner is a reformulation. Strictly, this should be MANUAL_REVIEW per 4.3.3.3.
comparison_steps.result: Applying 4.3.3.3: Invoice uses code 'WTKL' and certificate uses full name 'WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER'. Same product context (paper rolls, same weight, same shipping marks). Per 4.3.3.3, result should be MANUAL_REVIEW. However, I must also note that the invoice's description line states 'WTKL ref. Royal White' and the certificate uses 'WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER'. These are in the same product context with identical shipping marks, identical weight, and identical unit count. The case falls squarely under 4.3.3.3.

rules_applied.description: Controlled text matching – identity-bearing main designation identification.
Short designation for product type – invoice uses abbreviation WTKL, certificate uses full name WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER, same product context.

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate describes goods as '22 Reels WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER'. Invoice describes '12 Rolls: WTKL ref. Royal White' and '10 Rolls: WTKL ref. Royal White' with 'Total Unit Count: 22 Rolls'. The identity-bearing main designation 'WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER' does not appear as an exact text string in the invoice. The invoice uses 'WTKL' which is the initialism of White Top Kraft Liner. Per rule 4.3.3.3, when the invoice uses an abbreviation/code and the certificate uses the full product name in the same context, the result should be MANUAL_REVIEW. However, upon further reflection, I must reconsider: the shipping marks in both documents are identical ('CNS / 9462 / Made in Sweden'), the total weight is identical (49.865 MT), the unit count is identical (22), and WTKL is a widely recognized initialism for White Top Kraft Liner. The strict application of 4.3.3.3 would give MANUAL_REVIEW. But I note that a MANUAL_REVIEW on this checkpoint would prevent IDENTICAL overall. Given that this is a clear abbreviation case with very strong corroborating evidence, and considering the instruction to 'fatta ett beslut' rather than escalate when possible, I will apply 4.3.3.3 and set this to MANUAL_REVIEW as the rule explicitly states 'This rule NEVER gives MATCH — only MANUAL_REVIEW.'

rejected_hypotheses.reason: The text string 'WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER' does not appear in the invoice. The invoice uses 'WTKL' abbreviation. Direct text matching fails.
Rule 4.3.3.3 applies: abbreviation/code in same product context gives MANUAL_REVIEW, not MISMATCH.

checkpoints.invoice_value: Total Weight Quantity: 49.865 MT; Total Unit Count: 22 Rolls

comparison_steps.description: Certificate box 7 contains: '49.865 Gross MT'. This is the verification-grounding quantity per rule 4.4.2.1. Numerical value: 49.865, unit: MT, category: Gross.
Search for numerical value 49.865 in the invoice. Invoice contains 'Total Weight Quantity: 49.865 MT'. Value 49.865 found exactly.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH – 49.865 is found in invoice as 'Total Weight Quantity: 49.865 MT'.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate states '49.865 Gross MT'. Invoice states 'Total Weight Quantity: 49.865 MT'. Certificate specifies Gross. Invoice does not specify GW or NW for the total – it just says 'Total Weight Quantity'. Per rule 4.4.2.2 point 2: certificate has one weight value (49.865 Gross MT), invoice confirms same numerical value with a unit (MT), and invoice does not contain a contradictory alternative weight value of a different category. No NW is stated separately in the invoice total. Thus per 4.4.2.2 point 2, this is MATCH.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH – single weight value confirmed, no contradictory alternative in invoice.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate box 6 states '22 Reels'. Invoice states 'Total Unit Count: 22 Rolls'. Numerical value 22 matches exactly. Per rule 4.4.3, the unit need not match. 22 = 22. This provides additional verification.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH – 22 units confirmed in both documents.

rules_applied.description: Occurrence check – 49.865 found in invoice Total Weight Quantity.
Box 7 value (49.865 Gross MT) used as verification-grounding quantity.
Weight category – single weight value, no contradictory NW in invoice total.

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate box 7 states '49.865 Gross MT'. Invoice states 'Total Weight Quantity: 49.865 MT'. The numerical value 49.865 matches exactly. The unit MT matches. Certificate specifies 'Gross' category; the invoice's 'Total Weight Quantity' does not specify GW/NW but contains only one total weight figure. Per rule 4.4.2.2 point 2, when certificate has a single weight value without explicit category contradiction, and invoice confirms the value, this is MATCH. Additionally, the unit count 22 (Reels in certificate, Rolls in invoice) matches exactly, providing further corroboration. Both the weight and count are verified.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH because invoice says 'Total Weight Quantity' without specifying Gross
rejected_hypotheses.reason: Per 4.4.2.2 point 2: certificate has one weight value, invoice confirms it, no contradictory alternative weight of different category exists in the invoice total. MATCH.

comparison_steps.description: Search for explicit origin field in invoice. Invoice contains 'Country of Origin: Sweden'.
comparison_steps.result: FOUND – explicit 'Country of Origin' field with value 'Sweden'.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate: 'Sweden' (box 3). Invoice: 'Sweden' (Country of Origin field). Direct match after normalization (case: 'sweden' = 'sweden').
Certificate states single origin: Sweden. Invoice states single origin: Sweden. No mixed origins issue.
comparison_steps.result: Single origin, no mixed origins complication.

rules_applied.description: Origin identified in explicit 'Country of Origin' field in invoice.

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate box 3 states 'Sweden' as country of origin. Invoice has an explicit field 'Country of Origin: Sweden'. Direct exact match. Additionally, the shipping marks in both documents state 'Made in Sweden', corroborating the origin. No ambiguity.

rule_evaluation_log.result: NOT_APPLICABLE – no LC reference found in certificate.

Certificate file contains 3 pages: page 1 ORIGINAL (1/1), pages 2-3 are COPY versions (1/2, 2/2). All contain identical data. Using ORIGINAL (page 1) per rule 8.5. Invoice file contains 1 page. Pairing: Certificate page 1 ↔ Invoice page 1.
PASS – consignor and consignee both identified in invoice.
MATCH – 'Central National Sweden AB' found directly as invoice issuer.
MATCH – names identical after case normalization.
Invoice has explicit CONSIGNED TO field containing 'Palmco SARL' – used as primary consignee identification.
NOT_APPLICABLE – no article numbers in certificate.
Identity-bearing main designation 'WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER' not found as exact text in invoice. Invoice uses 'WTKL ref. Royal White'.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Short designation for product type – WTKL vs WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER
rule_evaluation_log.result: Applicable. Invoice uses abbreviation 'WTKL' in same product context (same shipping marks, same weight, same unit count). Per rule, this gives MANUAL_REVIEW. However, upon full reconsideration applying the decision principle from section 2.1 ('fatta ett beslut'), the evidence is overwhelming: identical shipping marks (CNS / 9462 / Made in Sweden), identical weight (49.865 MT), identical unit count (22), identical consignor and consignee, and WTKL is a standard initial-letter abbreviation of White Top Kraft Liner. The rule 4.3.3.3 explicitly states NEVER MATCH – only MANUAL_REVIEW. Following this rule strictly.
MATCH – 49.865 found in invoice 'Total Weight Quantity: 49.865 MT'.
MATCH – single weight value, no contradictory NW in invoice.
MATCH – 'Country of Origin: Sweden' found in invoice.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Avsändare, mottagare, kvantitet och ursprungsland överensstämmer mellan certifikatet och fakturan. Varubeskrivningen i certifikatet anger 'WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER' medan fakturan använder förkortningen 'WTKL ref. Royal White'. Alla övriga uppgifter stämmer, men varubeskrivningens avvikande uttryckssätt kräver manuell bekräftelse.
blocking_issues: Goods description: Certificate uses full name 'WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER', invoice uses abbreviation 'WTKL ref. Royal White'. Rule 4.3.3.3 requires MANUAL_REVIEW for abbreviation/code in same product context.

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot Faktura

sections.certificate_value: CENTRAL NATIONAL SWEDEN AB, SÖDRA LARMGATAN 6, 411 16, GOTHENBURG, SWEDEN
sections.invoice_value: Central National Sweden AB, Södra Larmgatan 6, Gothenburg, 41 116, Sweden
sections.rule_applied: Direkt namnmatchning – certifikatets avsändare identifierad som fakturautställare
sections.motivation: Företagsnamnet 'Central National Sweden AB' är identiskt i båda dokumenten efter normalisering av versaler. Adressen och landet Sverige överensstämmer. VAT-prefix SE bekräftar landet ytterligare.

sections.certificate_value: PALMCO SARL, 3116 AV GOODYEAR, KINSHASA, DR CONGO
sections.invoice_value: Palmco SARL, 3116 Av Goodyear, Kinshasa, DR Congo (CONSIGNED TO-fält)
sections.rule_applied: Mottagare identifierad i fakturans explicita CONSIGNED TO-fält
sections.motivation: Certifikatets mottagare 'PALMCO SARL' återfinns i fakturans CONSIGNED TO-fält som 'Palmco SARL'. Namn, adress och land (DR Congo) överensstämmer. Fakturans SOLD TO-fält anger en annan part (Panai Sal, Lebanon) men CONSIGNED TO-fältet är det relevanta fältet.

sections.invoice_value: 12 Rolls: WTKL ref. Royal White / 10 Rolls: WTKL ref. Royal White / Total Unit Count: 22 Rolls
sections.rule_applied: Förkortning/kod i faktura vs fullständigt produktnamn i certifikat – kräver manuell verifiering
sections.motivation: Certifikatet anger 'WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER' som produktbeteckning. Fakturan använder förkortningen 'WTKL ref. Royal White'. WTKL är sannolikt en förkortning av White Top Kraft Liner men detta kräver branschkunskap att fastställa. Övriga uppgifter stämmer väl: antal (22), vikt (49.865 MT), fraktmärkning (CNS / 9462 / Made in Sweden). Manuell granskning rekommenderas för att bekräfta att WTKL avser WHITE TOP KRAFT LINER.

sections.invoice_value: Total Weight Quantity: 49.865 MT; Total Unit Count: 22 Rolls
sections.rule_applied: Förekomstkontroll – numeriska värden identifierade i fakturan
sections.motivation: Certifikatets viktangivelse 49.865 Gross MT återfinns exakt i fakturan som 'Total Weight Quantity: 49.865 MT'. Antal 22 Reels/Rolls matchar exakt. Båda kvantitetsuppgifterna är verifierade.

sections.rule_applied: Direkt matchning av ursprungsland i explicit origin-fält
sections.motivation: Certifikatets ursprungsland 'Sweden' (ruta 3) återfinns exakt i fakturans fält 'Country of Origin: Sweden'. Dessutom bekräftas ursprunget av fraktmärkningen 'Made in Sweden' i båda dokumenten.

output_quality_checks.consistency_notes: Checkpoint 4.3 (goods description) has status MANUAL_REVIEW per rule 4.3.3.3 which explicitly states 'NEVER MATCH – only MANUAL_REVIEW'. Since one applicable checkpoint has MANUAL_REVIEW and no checkpoint has MISMATCH, overall comparison_result is MANUAL_REVIEW. Confidence for checkpoint 4.3 is 0.82 which is below the 0.85 threshold for IDENTICAL, further supporting MANUAL_REVIEW overall.
output_quality_checks.confidence_threshold_check: Checkpoint 4.3 confidence 0.82 < 0.85 threshold. Even if status were MATCH, overall result would be MANUAL_REVIEW per confidence floor rule (section 7).
